findings.title: GDPR Compliance Audit Blocker
findings.description: The PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch. Key concerns include lawful basis for processing behavioral data, adequacy of consent mechanisms, data retention policies, right to erasure implementation, and completion of a data protection impact assessment (DPIA). This is a critical blocker for EU market launch.
assumptions: GDPR compliance is mandatory for EU market entry.

evidence.excerpt: GDPR compliance audit request... Key concerns: (1) lawful basis for processing behavioral data...

recommendations: Complete the GDPR compliance audit before proceeding with any user profiling features.

findings.title: User Behavior Tracking Pipeline Dependency
findings.description: The implementation of the ML recommendation engine (PROF-102) is dependent on the successful build of the user behavior tracking pipeline (PROF-101). The pipeline must be operational to provide the necessary data for training the recommendation model.
assumptions: The user behavior tracking pipeline can handle the required event throughput.

evidence.excerpt: Implement a data ingestion pipeline that captures browsing history, click patterns...
Build a recommendation engine that uses accumulated user profile data...

recommendations: Prioritize the development of the user behavior tracking pipeline.

findings.title: Phased Delivery Plan
findings.description: Propose a phased delivery plan for the PersonaLens project. MVP should focus on the user behavior tracking pipeline (PROF-101) and GDPR compliance audit (PROF-106). V1 can include the ML recommendation engine (PROF-102) and user preference dashboard (PROF-103). V2 should incorporate cross-device tracking (PROF-104) and data export for advertisers (PROF-105).
assumptions: Phased delivery will allow for iterative testing and compliance checks.
recommendations: Implement the phased delivery plan as outlined.

findings.title: Privacy Risks in User Profiling
findings.description: There are significant privacy risks associated with the user profiling system, particularly concerning the handling of personal data and potential re-identification of users from anonymized data. Legal has flagged these concerns, especially in relation to the data export for advertisers (PROF-105).
assumptions: Anonymization processes must be robust to prevent user re-identification.

evidence.excerpt: The anonymization pipeline needs validation — legal has flagged that with enough cohort intersections...

recommendations: Enhance anonymization techniques and validate against legal requirements.

summary: The analysis identifies critical risks related to GDPR compliance and privacy in the PersonaLens user profiling project. A phased delivery plan is recommended, prioritizing the user behavior tracking pipeline and compliance audit in the MVP phase, followed by the recommendation engine and user dashboard in V1, and cross-device tracking and data export in V2.
